CREATE TABLE test_power (base INTEGER, exponent INTEGER, result DOUBLE PRECISION);
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (2, 2, power(2, 2));
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (3, 3, power(3, 3));
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (4, 1, power(4, 1));
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (5, 0, power(5, 0));
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (-2, 3, power(-2, 3));
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (-3, 2, power(-3, 2));
INSERT INTO test_power (base, exponent, result) VALUES (0, 4, power(0, 4));

CREATE PROCEDURE calc_exp_powers (in _from INTEGER, in _to INTEGER) { DECLARE i INTEGER; DECLARE result DOUBLE PRECISION; result_names(i, result); i := _from; WHILE (i <= _to) { result(i, power(i, 2)); i := i + 1; } };
SELECT base, exponent, result FROM test_power WHERE result IS NOT NULL;
SELECT base, exponent, result FROM test_power WHERE result > 10 ORDER BY result DESC;
CREATE TABLE additional_test (num INTEGER, new_result DOUBLE PRECISION);
INSERT INTO additional_test (num, new_result) SELECT num, power(num, 3) FROM (SELECT base FROM test_power) AS subquery WHERE base < 6;
SELECT a.base, p.result FROM test_power a JOIN additional_test p ON a.base = p.num WHERE p.new_result IS NULL;
